architectureGuardrails: Frontend calls FastAPI only; stories use deterministic mock FastAPI responses.
No Storybook story talks directly to NATS or JetStream.
No Storybook story executes shell commands.
Write actions remain represented as Typed Operations submitted through FastAPI endpoints.
Approval, rejection, resume, auto-approval, operation status, and audit behavior are documented as UI states rather than bypassed.

screens.purpose: Discover apps and services, refresh the catalog, choose install sources, and submit blueprint installs as typed operations.

states.normal: Catalog cards are loaded from the FastAPI catalog endpoint with health and event panels visible.
states.loading: Initial catalog fetch shows a loading treatment before cards are available.
states.empty: No apps are shown when the catalog endpoint returns an empty item list.
states.degraded: When FastAPI/NATS readiness is degraded, write actions are blocked and the UI explains that safe worker execution is required.
states.simple_mode: The screen title becomes Apps & Services and deploy actions read Install.
states.professional_mode: The professional screen exposes source mode, source reference, deployment phase, and task id details.
states.button_action_behavior: Refresh catalog posts catalog_refresh; Install/Deploy Workload submits deploy_blueprint.
states.operation_status_behavior: Queued, blocked, succeeded, and error phases are rendered through deployment status and SimpleStatus copy.

screens.purpose: Launch named update and validation tasks without exposing a command editor.

states.normal: Named tasks are selectable and the operation launcher is ready.
states.degraded: The launcher shows FastAPI/NATS degraded state and blocks non-preview writes.
states.permission_or_approval_required: Approval-required responses show the task as waiting for review instead of continuing locally.
states.failed_operation: Failed operation responses render in the task stream without fallback execution.
states.simple_mode: GitOps language becomes Keep My Environment Updated, Update Settings, Check for Changes, and Install.
states.professional_mode: Operation identifiers and named task mapping remain visible.
states.button_action_behavior: Run task calls previewOperation for previews or executeOperation for typed writes.
states.operation_status_behavior: The launcher stream records queued, approval, success, and failure details with job references.
states.backend_sync_behavior: Reads control-plane readiness and writes only through /api/operations/execute or /api/operations/preview.

screens.purpose: Add and monitor edge devices through typed fleet join and NATS-backed agent status views.

states.normal: Fleet nodes and agents are visible with live health-check controls.
states.empty: No live agents shows an explicit empty state.
states.degraded: Write actions explain that safe NATS/worker execution is unavailable.
states.success: A generated join payload is shown as an invite artifact after fleet_join is accepted.
states.simple_mode: Fleet Scaling becomes My Devices and Generate fleet_join becomes Add Device.
states.professional_mode: The join artifact and NATS-backed command details stay visible.
states.button_action_behavior: Add Device submits fleet_join; Save Network Key submits rotate_secret for Tailscale; health checks use FastAPI fleet routes.
states.operation_status_behavior: Invite, broadcast, and per-device check results are appended to the visible log stream.
states.backend_sync_behavior: Reads /api/fleet.json and /api/fleet/agents; writes through /api/operations/execute and fleet agent command routes.

screens.purpose: Rotate managed secrets and request short-lived access while keeping secret values hidden behind typed operations.

states.normal: Saved access and dynamic lease panels are visible.
states.error: Failed secret rotation or lease requests show controlled error copy.
states.success: Successful rotate_secret and secret_read_dynamic responses show task references and lease metadata.
states.degraded: Write actions are blocked when readiness is degraded.
states.simple_mode: Identity & Vault becomes Passwords & Access and Rotate Secret becomes Change Password.
states.professional_mode: Secret version, lease duration, and task ids remain visible for operators.
states.button_action_behavior: Change Password/Rotate Secret calls rotate_secret; temporary access calls secret_read_dynamic.
states.operation_status_behavior: Task references and lease status summarize queued/succeeded/failed outcomes.
states.backend_sync_behavior: Writes through /api/operations/execute; reads live events through the shared event panel.

screens.purpose: Show release stages, update checks, release apply flow, and subsystem evidence for safe self-update behavior.

states.normal: The workflow stage list, release status, subsystem bindings, and action history are visible.
states.loading: The screen announces that the release workflow is loading.
states.error: Unavailable release workflow data shows a controlled status line.
states.success: Accepted update check/apply commands add action history entries.
states.simple_mode: Release Workflow becomes Update Pocket Lab with Check Updates and Update Everything controls.
states.professional_mode: Subsystem bindings and operation identifiers remain visible.
states.button_action_behavior: Check release and Apply orchestrated release use release self-update FastAPI commands; stage buttons use typed operations.
states.operation_status_behavior: Stage status and action history show command ids, job ids, running state, and failures.
states.backend_sync_behavior: Reads /api/release/workflow and /api/release/self-update/status; writes check/apply through FastAPI.

screens.purpose: Detect, preview, approve, apply, or ignore drift using typed operation status and policy guardrails.

states.normal: Drift summary, selected job details, diff preview, and recent operation runs are visible.
states.permission_or_approval_required: Policy guardrail details identify when remediation waits for review or policy readiness.
states.failed_operation: Failed drift actions appear in the visible task message and operation stream.
states.degraded: Drift data and write action results fail closed without local simulation.
states.simple_mode: Drift Center becomes Health & Issues and Drift Detected becomes Something Changed.
states.professional_mode: Job ids, targets, diff JSON, and operation status details remain visible.
states.button_action_behavior: Scan, preview, approve, apply, and ignore call typed operation or FastAPI drift routes.
states.operation_status_behavior: Recent runs and task messages show pending approval, failed, and completed outcomes.
states.backend_sync_behavior: Reads /api/drift/summary, /api/drift/jobs, job diff endpoints, and /api/operations/runs; writes through typed operation and drift decision routes.

screens.purpose: Document safety checks and approved remediation actions.

states.button_action_behavior: Safety checks and approved fixes are typed operations.

screens.purpose: Document device resource and event-stream status.

states.degraded: Low disk or degraded health is visible without hiding system status.

screens.purpose: Document backup, restore, and verification states.

states.normal: Recent backup activity and recovery controls are visible.
states.button_action_behavior: Backup, restore, and verify are typed operations.

screens.purpose: Document OPA policy deployment, evaluation, and guardrail states.

states.normal: Policy evaluations and deployment controls are visible.
states.button_action_behavior: Policy deploy is submitted as a typed operation.

screens.purpose: Document the opt-in Enterprise Mode governance switch and Personal Mode auto-approval explanation.

states.enterprise_mode: Enterprise Mode requires human approval for governed runbooks.
states.normal: Personal Mode auto-approves eligible governed runbooks and logs audit evidence.
